raw_claim: The value function produced by a contracting Bellman-style operator is a fixed point of that operator.
notes: Remapped to v3 Foundations dynamic-programming entries.

raw_claim: Blackwell-style contraction conditions fit the contraction-mapping template that yields a fixed point.
notes: Remapped to v3 Foundations contraction and fixed-point entries.

raw_claim: Pointwise order on continuation values is preserved by Bellman operator monotonicity.
notes: Remapped to v3 Foundations Bellman monotonicity.

raw_claim: Continuous preference representations remain continuous on feasible subsets of the commodity space.
notes: Remapped to v3 Foundations continuity of preferences.

raw_claim: Convex preference representations induce convex utility maps on the full commodity space.
notes: Remapped to v3 Foundations convex-preference entry.

raw_claim: Constrained-optimization certificates combine feasibility with weak dominance over all feasible alternatives.
notes: Remapped to v3 Foundations constrained-optimization entry.

raw_claim: Kuhn-Tucker certificates package primal feasibility, dual feasibility, and complementary slackness in one reusable object.
notes: Remapped to v3 Foundations Kuhn-Tucker entry.

raw_claim: Witness-based equilibrium certificates are sufficient to conclude Nash equilibrium existence.
notes: Remapped to v3 Foundations Nash witness entry.

raw_claim: The canonical fixed point selected by the contraction theorem is indeed fixed.
notes: Remapped to v3 Foundations fixed-point theorem entry.

raw_claim: Policy improvement is reflexive and can seed iterative recursive arguments.
notes: Remapped to v3 Foundations policy-iteration entry.
